summary: Converted the project to use extern/dolphin instead of the repository's local Dolphin declarations/implementations. The PR required replacing forward-declared or made-up local types with proper Dolphin headers, moving locally invented vector-like structs such as S16Vec into mtx.h for the time being, and fixing multiply-defined extern/dolphin symbols that prevented linking. The decomp.dev bot reported no GALE01 output changes, but the author explicitly noted remaining non-matches in os and dvd against previous local versions.
what_changed: Switched the project to using extern/dolphin.
Removed or replaced local forward declarations and other locally maintained Dolphin-like definitions.
Changed code to include and depend on proper Dolphin headers instead of forward-declared types where possible.
Moved made-up local structs/types such as S16Vec into mtx.h as a temporary organization choice to avoid introducing a new header.
Fixed multiply-defined things in extern/dolphin that would otherwise prevent linking.
Resolved TODO items around #pragma clang complaints, gx struct linking, MTXQuat visibility for HSD_MtxSRTQuat, __DVDThreadQueue section placement, and OSReport stack-size differences.
Left various existing os and dvd non-matches unresolved, with previous versions reportedly stored under /todo/dolphin/.
smart_moves: Migrating to extern/dolphin centralizes Dolphin SDK-style definitions and reduces divergence from shared upstream headers.
Replacing forward declarations with proper headers is safer for matching work because it exposes real type layouts, declarations, and compiler-visible dependencies.
The author treated linker failures from duplicate extern/dolphin definitions as part of the migration rather than papering over them locally.
Temporarily placing S16Vec-like local compatibility types in mtx.h avoided creating a new header during a broad migration while preserving build progress.
The PR explicitly tracked migration blockers as TODO items, making it clear which failures were resolved and which os/dvd non-matches remained.
decomp_lessons: When adopting a shared Dolphin header source, expect hidden dependencies on proper includes to replace many forward declarations.
Forward declarations that were sufficient for a local decomp tree can break once real external headers expose fuller relationships between types.
Large dependency/header migrations may produce link-time issues even when compile-time type errors are fixed, especially from multiply-defined globals or internals.
Section placement still matters during library migration; __DVDThreadQueue needed to be in the linker-expected section.
Stack differences can arise from seemingly equivalent reporting functions such as OSReport, so matching verification must include stack-size checks.
A no-change GALE01 report is useful evidence that the broad header/dependency migration did not alter final output for the checked build, but it does not prove all library non-matches were solved.
naming_conventions: Uses extern/dolphin as the canonical external Dolphin dependency location.
Keeps Dolphin-style names such as S16Vec, MTXQuat, HSD_MtxSRTQuat, __DVDThreadQueue, and OSReport rather than renaming them to project-specific variants.
The PR text refers to the internal gx struct in lowercase quotes as "gx", suggesting care was needed around extern/dolphin's own internal naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use decomp.dev GALE01 reports to confirm whether a broad header/dependency migration changes final output; this PR's bot report said 'No changes'.
Compare migrated os and dvd code against saved previous versions when non-matches remain; the author noted a /todo/dolphin/ folder with prior versions.
Investigate stack-size differences around OSReport as a matching-sensitive area.
Check linker section placement for globals such as __DVDThreadQueue during extern/dolphin migration.
Resolve undefined-type complaints such as MTXQuat in HSD_MtxSRTQuat by fixing include/header visibility rather than inventing duplicate declarations.
review_feedback: No substantive human review comments were captured in the available slice.
A dismissed review by ribbanya is present with an empty body, so no actionable reviewer rationale is available.
decomp-dev bot reported: 'Report for GALE01 ... No changes'.
The diff was not available in the slice because GitHub returned HTTP 406: diff exceeded the maximum number of files, so file-level evidence is weak.

Look for later PRs that clean up the temporary S16Vec placement or resolve the os/dvd non-matches left in this PR.
